sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(100), score INT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, score, created_at) VALUES (1, 'abc', 50, NOW()), (2, 'def', NULL, NOW()), (3, '', 0, NOW());
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE like(name, CONCAT('a%', 'b%'), '#', false) AND (score >> 1) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, name, score >> 2 AS shifted_score FROM test_table WHERE (like(name, 'd%', '#', false) OR score IS NULL) GROUP BY id HAVING shifted_score > 10;
CREATE TABLE secondary_table (id INT, ref_id INT, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO secondary_table (id, ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'description1'), (2, 2, NULL), (3, 1, 'description3');

SELECT t.id, t.name, t.score FROM test_table t JOIN secondary_table s ON t.id = s.ref_id AND (like(s.description, 'desc%', '#', false) OR s.description IS NULL) WHERE t.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' ORDER BY (string.length(t.name) >> 1) DESC;
SELECT t.id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY like(t.name, 'a%', '#', false)) AS match_count FROM test_table t GROUP BY t.id HAVING match_count > 1;
SELECT avg(score) AS average_score FROM test_table WHERE (score IS NOT NULL AND (score >> 1) > 0) AND (like(name, '%abc%', '#', false) OR name = '');
CREATE TABLE temp_table (temp_id INT, temp_name VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO temp_table (temp_id, temp_name) VALUES (1, 'temp_abc'), (2, NULL);
SELECT DISTINCT t.temp_id FROM temp_table t WHERE (t.temp_name IS NULL AND (1 >> t.temp_id) > 0) OR (like(t.temp_name, 'temp%', '#', false) AND t.temp_name != '');
